ocr.text: In recent years, the Asia-Pacific region has experienced notable shifts in student financing dynamics, underscored by the
increasing student loan default rates and corresponding tuition revenue metrics.

9.5%  4.2%  1.3MKRW  TotalDefaults  TotalStudents  AverageLoanSize  LoanRepaymentRate  TotalScholarships  TotalGrants  TotalDefaults  TotalStudents

As reflected in the data, the default rate among students rose from 6.2% in 2021 to 7.1% in 2022, highlighting a growing
concern around student debt sustainability. Concurrently, the tuition revenue per student also saw an increase, with figures
escalating from ₩1,200,000 in 2020 to ₩1,250,000 in 2021.
Year  TuitionRevenue  PerStudent  2020Data  2021Data  2022Data  GrowthRate  Region  Organization  TotalRevenue
Asia-Pacific  Pearsonplc  ₩540,000,000  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  ₩1,300,000  4.2%  Asia-Pacific  Pearsonplc  ₩500,000,000  2021  ₩1,250,000  ₩1,300,000  ₩1,350,000
This trend raises significant questions regarding the affordability of education and the economic pressures faced by students,
particularly those from low-income backgrounds, where the default rate reached 10.5%, slightly below the anticipated 11.0%.
Furthermore, total students enrolled in higher education institutions increased alongside these financial metrics, with the total
student population estimated at 12.0% growth. As institutions adapt to these changing financial landscapes, the implications for
educational policy and funding models will be critical for ensuring equitable access to higher education. For instance, the
overall amount lent in student loans reached a significant benchmark, contributing to the total amount lent which recorded as
1.2MKRW. Understanding these interconnected factors will be essential for stakeholders aiming to foster a sustainable
educational environment in the region.
